steps.title: Track the two-part structure
steps.content: The text first reports that others regard Mr. Harford favorably. It then lists his cautious behaviors, such as avoiding warm discussion and not letting others laugh at him.

steps.title: Match opinion to behavior
steps.content: A invents a contrast with London. B claims a public-private discrepancy and hidden motive, and C invents a changed reputation. The structure links others' favorable opinion to the conduct that preserves it. The answer is D.
